WILLIAM (-killed in battle Acre 1190 before 21 Oct).  “Willielmus comes de Ferrariis” donate …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
William II de Ferrers, 4th Earl of Derby (c. 1168-c. 1247), was a favourite of King John of England. He succeeded to the estate (but not the title) upon the death of his father, William de Ferrers, 3rd Earl of Derby, at the Siege of Acre in 1190. He was head of a family which controlled a large part of Derbyshire which included an area known as Duffield Frith.

«b»Life«/b»
He adopted his father's allegiance to King Richard as the reigning king. On Richard's return from the Third Crusade, in the company of David Ceannmhor and the Earl of Chester he played a leading role in besieging Nottingham Castle, on 28 March 1194, which was being held by supporters of Prince John. For seven weeks after this he held the position of Sheriff of Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire.

On the accession of John after the death of his brother, in 1199, William gave him his allegiance, and became a great favourite. He restored to the de Ferrars' family the title of Earl of Derby, along with the right to the "third penny", and soon afterwards bestowed upon him the manors of Ashbourne and Wirksworth, with the whole of that wapentake, subject to a fee farm rent of £70 per annum.

When, in 1213, John surrendered his kingdoms of England and Ireland to the Pope, William was one of the witnesses to the "Bulla Aurea." In the following year William gave surety on behalf of the king for the payment of a yearly tribute of 1,000 marks.

In the same year, 1214, the King granted the Earl the royal castle of Harestan (Horsley Castle). William was a patron of at least 2 abbeys and 4 priories. In 1216, John made him bailiff of the Peak Forest and warden of the Peak Castle.

In that year, John was succeeded by the nine-year-old Henry III. Because of continuing discontent about John's violations of the Magna Carta, some of the barons had approached Prince Louis of France who invaded in that year. William Marshal, 1st Earl of Pembroke acting on behalf of the young King sought to repel the invaders and pacify the barons. His forces, with the assistance of de Ferrers, the Earl of Chester and others, defeated the rebels at the siege of Lincoln.

De Ferrers was allowed to retain the royal castles of Bolsover, Peak and Horston (Horsley) until the King's 14th birthday. The latter had been given him in 1215 as a residence for his wife, during his planned absence with the King on Crusade and the Earl was among those who made representation to the King, which would in 1258 led to the Provisions of Oxford .

Henry reached his fourteenth birthday in 1222 and his administration sought to recover the three royal castles, to de Ferrers' indignation. In 1254 they would pass to Edward I, Henry's son, exacerbating Robert's, the sixth earl, resentment against the prince.

He married Agnes of Chester, sister of Ranulph de Blondeville, 4th Earl of Chester, for 55 years. As the Earl advanced in years he became a martyr to severe attacks of the gout, a disease which terminated his life in the year 1247. He was succeeded by his elder son, also William, the Fifth Earl of Derby.

«b»William de Ferrers School«/b»
William de Ferrers School and Sixth form is a "foundation comprehensive" (state-funded, non-selective, with some control over how to spend its allotted money) school in the rural town of South Woodham Ferrers, Essex. The school is named after William Ferrers a descendant of Henry de Ferrers who was given the area as a gift from William the Conqueror after the Norman Conquest.

«b»William De Ferrers Football Club«/b»
Henry Ferrers' descendant gave his name to the local Essex (UK) football team of the same name, often abbreviated to Willy De or known simply as The Baby blues. The club was founded in 1983 and currently has 3 senior men's teams.

«b»Family and children«/b»
1.) William de Ferrers, 5th Earl of Derby

2.) Sir Thomas de Ferrers, of Chartley Ferrers.

3.) Sir Hugh de Ferrers, of Bugbrooke, Northamptonshire, died shortly before 10 August 1257. He married _____, sister of Roger de Mohaut, Steward of Chester. They had one daughter, Cecily de Ferrers, wife of John de Oddingseles and Godfrey de Beaumont, Knt., of Drayton and Scarning, Norfolk, Grimston, Suffolk, etc.). See Willis, Estate Book of Henry de Bray (c.1289-1340).

4.) Robert de Ferrers.

5.) Ranulph de Ferrers, parson of St. Michael's on the Wyre, Lancashire.

6.) Bertha de Ferrers, married (1st) Thomas de Furnival, of Worksop, Nottinghamshire, and Sheffield, Yorkshire; (2nd) Ralph le Bigod, Knt., of Settrington, Yorkshire.

7.) Agnes de Ferrers, married Richard de Montfitchet.

8.) Sybil de Ferrers, married Sir John de Vipont, of Appleby and Brough under Stainmoor, Westmorland

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ferrers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire (Eure) and Ferrières (Manche).

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferridge
meanings: English: unexplained; possibly a form of Ferris and therefore a variant of Ferrers .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby was born in 1162, in Derbyshire, England, his father, William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby, was 26 and his mother, Sybil de Braose, was 28. He married Agnes of Chester in 1192. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 22 September 1247, in Evington, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby est né en 1162, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Sybil de Braose, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Agnes of Chester en 1192. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 22 septembre 1247, à Evington, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby 1162 in Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby 26 und seine Mutter Sybil de Braose 28. Er hat 1192 Agnes of Chester geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 22. September 1247 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Evington, Leicestershire, England und wurde in Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby è nato il nel 1162 nel Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Sybil de Braose, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Agnes of Chester nel 1192. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 22 settembre 1247, nel Evington, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolto a Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derbyが1162年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derbyは26歳，母Sybil de Braoseは28歳であった。1192年にAgnes of Chesterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1247年9月22日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアエヴィントンで85歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアチャートリー カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby(이)가 1162년 영국 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Sybil de Braose(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1192년 Agnes of Chester(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1247년 9월 22일 영국 레이스스터실르 에빙턴에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 처틀리 카스틀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby nasceu em 1162, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Sybil de Braose, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes of Chester em 1192. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de setembro de 1247, em Evington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby родился в 1162 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil de Braose, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes of Chester в 1192 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 22 сентября 1247 г. в местности эвингтон, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности чартли кастл, стаффордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Ferrers 4th Earl of Derby nació en 1162, en Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Ferrers 3rd Earl of Derby, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Sybil de Braose, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Agnes of Chester en 1192. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 22 de septiembre de 1247, en Evington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Chartley Castle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
